tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All fail-to-pass tests (test_scroll_uses_viewport_check, test_header_shows_date_when_expanded, test_collapse_animation_uses_size_transition, test_collapsible_section_top_alignment, test_no_duplicate_date_in_body) are purely text-matching: they read source files and assert on string presence/absence (e.g., 'assert "RenderAbstractViewport.maybeOf" in src'). No test compiles the Dart code, runs it, or checks runtime behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests check for specific code patterns that don't exist in the base commit (e.g., 'final isExpanding', '_CollapsibleBody', 'SizeTransition', 'Alignment.topCenter'). A stub or no-op implementation wouldn't contain these strings, so the tests would fail. The tests require actual code changes to pass.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution/solve.sh file exists in the container and contains the exact git commit SHA (1da86998cfa8f8bd27483b8e698fd38d88e6193f) to checkout. An agent could read /solution/solve.sh or simply run it to pass all tests. Additionally, since tests are purely text-matching, the agent could grep the test file for expected strings and insert them into the source files.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All five behavioral requirements from instruction.md (viewport-aware scroll, header date display, SizeTransition animation, AnimatedSize top alignment, no duplicate date) have corresponding test functions that check for them.
